sentence: We lived in Wales then.

sentence: Stonehenge is in the south of England.

sentence: The children stood in a circle.

sentence: The stone is very heavy.

sentence: The Romans built Hadrian's Wall.
sentenceGerman: Die Römer bauten den Hadrianswall.

sentence: The north is colder than the south.
sentenceGerman: Der Norden ist kälter als der Süden.

sentence: It takes two hours by train.
sentenceGerman: Mit dem Zug dauert es zwei Stunden.

sentence: The sun rises in the east.

sentence: The sun sets in the west.

sentence: The Vikings went to York.

sentence: The Vikings invaded York.

sentence: Denmark is in the north of Europe.

sentence: Norway is famous for its fjords.
sentenceGerman: Norwegen ist für seine Fjorde berühmt.

sentence: The two boys fought after school.

sentence: There is lots of industry in England.

sentence: I ate my lunch during the break.

sentence: The Industrial Revolution was from 1780 to 1840.

sentence: More than half a million people live there.

sentence: The sun is shining today.

sentence: She isn't at school because she is sick.
sentenceGerman: Sie ist nicht in der Schule, weil sie krank ist.

sentence: People found graves near Stonehenge.
sentenceGerman: Die Menschen fanden Gräber nahe Stonehenge.
